MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-10-293378 The Juanita B. Kelley Survivor's Trust and PTR-11-295040 The Exemption Trust Created Under The Kelley Family Trust.

Appearances: Sheila Robello for Gene Marchi (Trustee).

With respect to the $91,000.00 transfer from the Survivor's Trust to the Exemption Trust, Ms. Robello stated that the offsets were calculated when doing the first account to equalize the differences in rents and how funds were paid on the McAllister property, and for making sure that the three Brothers who purchased the property contributed the right shares to the Trust plus additional funds to make sure all shares were correct. Ms. Robello also stated that there was a Court order to stop payment on the storage unit, that they had difficulty getting Francine Kelley to pick up the contents, that they have spoken with her and her previous Counsel, and that she has not provided a receipt. In addition, Ms. Robello explained that File and Serve is used for e-filing and Nor Cal is used for delivery of courtesy copies, that Nor Cal was paid three times for delivery of an order that required revised language, that the ex parte petition to increase bond was a billing error and was actually filed in the Survivor's Trust, and that nobody has indicated any objections. The Second and Final Accounts and Reports of Successor Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-10-293378 Juanita B. Kelley Survivor's trust and PTR-11-295040 the Kelley Exemption Trust.
Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Gene Marchi. Seth Skootsky for (beneficiaries) Anthony, Reginald & Stan Kelley. Ann Draper for Anthony Kelley. David Parnall for Francine Kennedy. Brandy Estelle (via court call) for and with Francine Kennedy. John Rueppel, Guardian ad Litem for Brian Carideo.

The Court stated that we here regarding the follow up on the settlement agreements, the first and final report in the exemption trust and the first report in the survivors trust, that it has received objections regarding storage costs and asked if there were any other objections. Mr. Parnall stated that they do not object to the underlying petition, but do object to the proposed order regarding distribution of property instead of repayment of loans and that they would like a chance to comment in writing to the order. Mr. Skootsky stated that they did not receive a copy until yesterday, that they want the issue resolved, that the record shows that Francine Kennedy wanted a professional fiduciary as her brothers did not get along, that the only person who has had objections is Ms. Kennedy, that the professional fiduciary had a plan that treated everyone the same with the same opportunity, that it was delayed at Ms. Kennedy's request and then she could not make it, that after October 2014 the only person with an interest in the property was Ms. Kennedy, that she now objects to storage fees and that it is not appropriate for her to request substituted judgment. Ms. Robello stated that the issue in ongoing, that there has been no success regarding payment or clearance of the storage unit and that there needs to be a cutoff when people do not act. Ms. Estelle stated that the underlying facts are irrelevant, that the storage unit is an issue because the property was sold over objections of Ms. Kennedy, that the Kelley brothers blocked her from dealing with the issue and that the fees should be split by all parties. Ms. Draper stated that Mr. Marchi was to be allowed to dispose of the property and that they need a compliance date. The Court stated that if the client does not want to pay fees and does not want it cleared out she needs to take care of it and that the successor trustee should not be made responsible. The court took the accounting petitions (both cases) under submission stating that it will make a determination regarding the storage unit matter, requested that the parties meet and confer regarding the proposed order and submit any written objections as to form with the order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with Case No. PTR-10-293378 (The Matter Of The Juanita B. Kelley Survivor's Trust Created Under The Kelley Family Trust dated 7/29/98)

Appearances: Emily Calhoun for Francene Kelley. Lawrence Siracusa for Rena Harel. John Rueppel (Guardian ad Litem) for Brian Carideo. Seth Skootsky for Anthony/Reginald/Stanley Kelley (Former Co-Trustee's/Beneficiaries). Nelson Wilde (Guardian ad Litem) for Kelly Ann Kelley.
**Disclosure by Judge Peter Busch: at one time he was assigned to the SF Law & Motion department and during that time in Law & Motion he supervised Mr. Rueppel, that he knows Mr. Rueppel professionally only, and that they have run into each other one or two times outside the court. No objections by counsel with regard to the Court's disclosure and proceeding with the matter.** The Court proceeded with Mr. Rueppel's petition for fees, recapped the petitions/issues that were before it today, and stated that Mr. Rueppel's fees were and rate was too high especially with his experience. Mr. Skootsky argued that Mr. Rueppel had only been part of the State Bar for 18 months and that Mr. Rueppel's lack of experience led to deficiencies. Mr. Rueppel argued in support of his fees noting that he did the bulk of the work with regard to the 4th Amendment contest. Mr. Wild stated that he wasn't sure that he was "lead" in response to a statement by counsel and that Mr. Rueppel did spend more time on other areas such as the the deposition of Bruce Friedman, etc. Additional argument by Mr. Skootsky. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the matter under submission. The Court proceeded with Francine Kennedy's fee petition. The Court expressed concerns with Ms. Kennedy's fee petition especially since Ms. Harel was available at the time, that Ms. Kennedy was appointed special admnistrator in the related Estate matter, and that their were concerns with regard to Ms. Kennedy and her own interests. Mr. Skootsky presented argument in opposition to the petition stating that there is a provision in the settlement agreement that limits the Trustee's fees and that the requested fees should be denied in their entirety. Discussions held regarding the language in the agreement. Mr. Skootsky stated that the language was never meant to expand fee requests and that Ms. Kennedy should have obtained the Court's blessing before going forward with the intra-litigation but she didn't. Ms. Calhoun presented argument in support of Ms. Kennedy's fee petition. Mr. Siracusa stated that Judge Wiss had requested a unified petition be filed which would be supplemented by declarations, addressed the bond, stated that he had not asked for fees at this time, and noted that Ms. Harel has resigned pending someone else being appointed per the settlement agreement. Mr. Wild addressed the Court. Mr. Skootsky presented additional argument. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the matter under submission.
